availability.summary: A live POST probe on 2026-05-10 returned HTTP 200 JSON with funding rows and page metadata, confirming the endpoint is currently callable.

parameters.description: Award identifier to inspect. Treat this request field as the endpoint's carrier for canonical_award_lookup_id: if you are chaining from v2__search__spending_by_award, pass generated_internal_id unchanged here; if you are chaining from v2__awards__award_id, pass generated_unique_award_id unchanged. Documentation prefers generated natural award ids but also allows deprecated numeric surrogate award ids for backward compatibility.

documented.notes: Docs describe either a generated natural award id string or a numeric surrogate award id. Generated ids are preferred because they are effectively permanent.

observed.notes: Observed working as a string identifier in a live POST. Reviewer-backed drilldown evidence also confirmed CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE- carried successfully from search generated_internal_id into this request body field.

constraints: Treat award_id as the endpoint's request-field carrier for canonical_award_lookup_id.
When chaining from search or award detail workflows, reuse canonical_award_lookup_id unchanged rather than substituting the display Award ID.
Generated award identifiers are preferred over numeric surrogate ids.

parameters.description: Maximum number of funding records to return per page.

observed.notes: A live request with limit=4 returned four rows.

parameters.description: 1-based results page to return for the selected award.

observed.notes: A reviewer-confirmed safe-template request used page=1 and returned HTTP 200 with page_metadata.page=1.

parameters.description: Primary safe pattern for ordering funding records: set sort explicitly whenever row order matters. The documented enum is usable and reviewer-backed safe-template evidence confirmed explicit sort=reporting_fiscal_date works; the contradiction concerns only the service default when sort is omitted.

documented.notes: Docs say object_class sorts by object class code/name, program_activity sorts by code/name, and reporting_fiscal_date sorts by reporting fiscal year/month.

observed.defaultBehavior: With sort omitted, live results were grouped by account_title descending, and within the same account the 2017 row appeared before the 2022 row instead of reporting_fiscal_date descending.
observed.notes: Explicit sort=reporting_fiscal_date with order=desc was reviewer-confirmed in a successful safe-template drilldown call. Set sort explicitly whenever deterministic ordering matters.

constraints: Prefer the safe explicit-sort template whenever pagination order, comparisons, or presentation order matter.
Use one documented sort value when deterministic ordering matters.
Treat omitted sort as unsafe because live/default behavior contradicts the documentation.
The contradiction is about the omitted default behavior, not about explicit documented sort values.

parameters.description: Sort direction for the selected sort key. Pair this with an explicit sort field in the safe request pattern rather than relying on omitted-sort defaults.

documented.notes: Docs define asc for ascending and desc for descending.

constraints: Prefer supplying order together with explicit sort for deterministic pagination and comparisons.

observed.notes: A reviewer-confirmed safe-template request used order=desc and returned HTTP 200.

templates.name: basic-award-funding-page
templates.description: Fetch the first funding page for one award using canonical_award_lookup_id under request body field award_id, with a small page size.

templates.name: safe-template-award-funding-page
templates.description: Primary safe template for one page of funding records using canonical_award_lookup_id under request body field award_id plus explicit pagination and ordering so callers do not rely on contradicted omitted-sort behavior.

response.shapeSummary: JSON object containing a results array of award funding rows and a page_metadata object; a live probe returned four rows for one award and page_metadata showing no next page for that sample.

fields.description: Funding-record rows for the requested award.
Pagination metadata for the current page of results.
Fiscal year associated with the reporting submission date for the funding row.
Fiscal quarter associated with the reporting submission date for the funding row.
Fiscal month associated with the reporting submission date for the funding row.
Whether the underlying submission is quarterly instead of monthly.
Raw disaster or emergency code associated with the funding row; treat it as a code/string-or-null field rather than a boolean-like value.

documented.notes: Docs describe this field as a code but type it as boolean.

observed.notes: Live rows returned actual null and the string code "Q"; the string "null" in acceptedValues is a schema-safe marker for observed nulls.

constraints: Do not coerce this field to boolean.

fields.description: Internal surrogate identifier of the awarding agency.
Top-tier awarding agency identifier for the funding row.
Slug for the awarding agency.
Name of the awarding agency.
Internal surrogate identifier of the funding agency.
Top-tier funding agency identifier for the funding row.
Slug for the funding agency.
Name of the funding agency.
Federal account identifier associated with the funding row.
Federal account title associated with the funding row.
Program activity code associated with the funding row.
Program activity name associated with the funding row.
Object class code associated with the funding row.
Object class name associated with the funding row.
Obligated amount associated with the funding row.
Gross outlay amount associated with the funding row.

observed.notes: The live sample returned null values, confirming callers should handle nulls.

fields.description: Current page number.
Whether another results page is available after the current one.
Whether a previous results page is available before the current one.
Next page number when another page exists; null otherwise.
Previous page number when a prior page exists; null otherwise.
1-based page selector in the request body.

observed.notes: A reviewer-confirmed safe-template request used page=1 and the response echoed page_metadata.page=1.

fields.description: Requested page size in the request body.
Current page number echoed in page_metadata.
Next page number when more results exist.
Previous page number when an earlier page exists.
True when another page is available.
True when a previous page is available.

pagination.notes: Pagination uses request-body page and limit controls plus response page_metadata next/previous flags; neither docs nor the current raw profile expose a total result count.

notes: Current raw profile appears to expose every documented top-level request field.
The remaining semantic gaps are contradiction handling and business caveats rather than missing top-level request fields.

contradictions.statement: When sort is omitted, documentation says the default is reporting_fiscal_date, but the live baseline response was ordered by account_title descending and showed 2017 before 2022 within the same account, contradicting a reporting_fiscal_date descending default.
contradictions.impact: Treat omitted sort as unsafe for deterministic pagination, comparisons, or presentation order. Set both sort and order explicitly; reviewer-backed safe-template usage confirmed explicit reporting_fiscal_date sorting works.

contradictions.statement: Documentation types disaster_emergency_fund_code as boolean, but the live response returned null and the string code 'Q'.
contradictions.impact: Treat this field as a raw code/string-or-null value rather than a boolean until corroborated by DEFC reference material.

quirks.statement: The current raw profile reports that unknown award ids return HTTP 200 with an empty results array rather than an explicit not-found error, but this specific bundle did not re-probe that behavior on 2026-05-10.
quirks.impact: Callers should treat empty results as either a legitimate no-data case or a missing-award case and validate the award id separately when needed.

quirks.statement: The live sample contained null gross_outlay_amount values while still returning valid rows.
quirks.impact: Aggregations should handle null outlay amounts explicitly rather than assuming every row has a numeric outlay value.

gaps.statement: Documentation does not provide total result counts, and the current raw profile also notes there is no total-count pagination metadata.
gaps.impact: Clients must paginate iteratively using hasNext or next instead of precomputing a last page.

gaps.statement: The meaning of any disaster_emergency_fund_code letter values is not explained in the endpoint docs.
gaps.impact: Agents should preserve the raw field and avoid hard-coding business meaning without corroborating DEFC reference material.

risks.statement: Related award-search, award-detail, and award-funding endpoints relabel the same generated award business key: search emits generated_internal_id, award detail echoes generated_unique_award_id, and this endpoint accepts the same value as request field award_id.
risks.impact: Normalize the handoff value as canonical_award_lookup_id and pass it unchanged when chaining across award drilldown endpoints.

risks.statement: Funding rows are period-specific accounting slices rather than deduplicated award totals, and results can be sparse enough to diverge sharply from headline award totals. In the reviewer-backed HT940216C0001 drilldown, award detail reported total_obligation 51269205263.03 while funding returned one 321840 row with hasNext false.
risks.impact: Use this endpoint for funding composition and time-sliced accounting context, not as a single authoritative award total. If you compare to award detail, check whether total_account_obligation is the closer benchmark before expecting reconciliation to total_obligation.
